name: MovingPandas (Trajectory Analysis for GeoPandas)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD-2-Clause); Anita Graser / AIT Austrian Institute of Technology

performance.notes: GeoPandas-backed; performance scales with trajectory count and point density

agent_readiness.notes: Trajectory analysis framework for GPS/movement data. import movingpandas as mpd. traj = mpd.Trajectory(gdf, traj_id_col='id', t='timestamp'). traj.get_speed(). traj.get_direction(). traj.add_speed(). traj.add_direction(). traj.get_length(). traj.get_duration(). traj.clip(polygon). traj.generalize(tolerance=0.001). TrajectoryCollection: mpd.TrajectoryCollection(gdf, 'id', t='time'). Splitters: mpd.StopSplitter, mpd.TemporalSplitter. Visualization: traj.plot(). hvPlot integration.

gotchas: Niche library — only for trajectory/movement data
Requires datetime-indexed GeoDataFrame with Point geometry
Performance degrades significantly with thousands of trajectories
Limited integration with modern geospatial stack (e.g., no DuckDB-Spatial)
best_when: GPS trajectory analysis (vehicle tracking, animal movement, pedestrian data)
Stop detection and trajectory segmentation from movement data
Speed and direction computation along trajectories
avoid_when: Large-scale trajectory processing (use custom Spark/Dask solution)
summary: MovingPandas provides trajectory analysis for GPS/movement data. mpd.Trajectory(gdf, traj_id_col='id'). .get_speed(), .get_direction(), .clip(polygon), .generalize(). TrajectoryCollection for multi-entity. StopSplitter, TemporalSplitter. BSD-2 by Anita Graser/AIT. Best for GPS track analysis, vehicle/animal movement, stop detection. Requires datetime-indexed GeoDataFrame. Limited scalability for thousands of trajectories.
